mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >70% of instances following a >0.2pp UNRATE jump, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear outcomes with p<0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p>0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average confirms robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which supports equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO above its 12-month MA coincides with negative 20-day SPX returns in >65% of observations during recovery phases, or the signal predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL > 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.4% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of high-inflation months, or the threshold fails to generate a statistically significant negative alpha (t-stat < -1.96).

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS/UNRATE signal yields negative 60-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth decelerating below 1.5% annualized signals weakening aggregate demand, which reduces corporate profit expectations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in >70% of quarters where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5%, or the signal shows no predictive edge during late-cycle phases (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding real output, which improves corporate earnings momentum and generates positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth correlates negatively with 5-day SPX returns in >60% of months, or the signal out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5 over rolling 3-year windows.
